title: Doe model runtime receipt
description: Binds execution manifest + host-plan + memory-plan + runtime-config + per-kernel CSL runtime fixtures into one artifact that answers 'can Doe run this model end-to-end'.

streamingMigration.description: Derived fields that decide whether the simple memcpy RPC runtime path is sufficient or whether the SdkLayout streaming runtime (priority #6 — code regions, ports, streams, async send/receive, demux/mux, KV spill) must be adopted for this model. Computed from the memory plan + model config; no new memory-plan schema fields are required upstream.

maxPerLayerStage.description: Largest stream stage with repeatCount > 1 (per-transformer-layer). Double-buffering targets this stage.

maxSetupStage.description: Largest one-shot stream stage (repeatCount == 1). Loaded at init; not double-buffered.

laneStatus.description: Structural coverage — does every host-plan kernel resolve to a runtime-ready fixture, and does the memory plan fit? 'structural_full_coverage' does NOT mean the model executed end-to-end; that is captured separately in executionStatus. Kept alongside the legacy values so older receipts stay parseable.

executionStatus.description: Has this model been executed end-to-end through the simulator or hardware? Structural full-coverage is a precondition but not a proof of execution. 'real_weight_layer_block_success' is a stronger simulator-status variant: same as simulator_success, but the run consumed real Gemma-4 weight slices (syntheticWeightsAbsent=true) and the weightSetSha256 matched the fixture pin.

executionBlocker.description: When executionStatus=not_attempted, names the missing prerequisite so follow-up work is explicit. 'none' means the model is ready to be executed. 'sdk_layout_streaming_executor_missing' and 'streaming_executor_not_bound_to_execution_plan' are legacy. The streaming-executor primitives exist on simfabric, and the layer-block runner now executes real pre-attn RMSNorm + scalar attention-inner-product + residual + post-attn RMSNorm + a gated MLP ReLU/GELU stub with all 3 streams flowing through compute. 'real_weights_absent' means the path to real_weight_layer_block_success is gated only on materialization of the --weights-dir contract.

realWeightEvidence.description: Optional block. When the external checkpoint-weight promotion artifact exists (not just synthetic data), this pins the fixture contract the run consumed, the weights-dir audit that validated the slice files, and the parity verdict that compared outputs across runtime lanes. Populated only when executionStatus is real_weight_layer_block_success or when executionBlocker names a real-weight-specific reason.

promotionCriteriaMet.description: Mirror of the parity verdict's 5 real-weight promotion criteria on the model-runtime receipt so downstream consumers see the honest status without cross-loading the parity file.

streamingExecutorPrimitivesEvidence.description: Optional pointer block. When the SdkLayout streaming executor primitives (stream I/O, compute transform, region-to-region chain, multi-PE SPMD, compile cache, layer-block-shaped chain) have been proven on simfabric, this field lists where the traces live. Lets downstream consumers (e.g. the demo status page) show 'streaming executor primitives: pass' as a distinct row from 'full-model execution: blocked'.

layerBlockKernelEvidence.description: Positive simulator evidence for the generated E2B layer-block smoke runner. This does not by itself promote model-level executionStatus; it records which real layer stages have run under simfabric and what remains before a full model-level simulator_success claim is honest.

fullGridCompileProbeEvidence.description: Optional pointer to the cslc grid-probe aggregate documenting which PE counts cslc accepts for this arch. Captured once per repo; referenced by every model receipt so downstream consumers can correlate 'this model wants NN PEs' against 'cslc has been proven to handle up to MM PEs'.

chainParityEvidence.description: Binds model-level chain-parity receipts to this model. Each chain-parity receipt proves that N specific kernels (of the model's host-plan patterns) compose end-to-end with bit-exact or bit-close numerical parity against numpy. The derived kernelPatternsChainProven array lists every classifier outcome that has appeared in at least one passing chain — it's the bridge between per-kernel parity (each kernel alone matches numpy) and model-level parity (the whole graph matches numpy).

kernelPatternsChainProven.description: Host-plan kernel patterns that appear in at least one passing chain-parity receipt.

kernelPatternsChainUnproven.description: Host-plan kernel patterns for which no chain-parity evidence exists yet.
